title_en: Cities are rethinking what happens after dark
title_zh: 城市正在重新思考夜幕降临后会发生什么
rubric_en: Making it easier to party is becoming serious public policy
rubric_zh: 让人们更容易尽兴聚会，正成为严肃的公共政策

paragraphs.en: THE JOB OF nightlife czar sounds almost hedonistic. Then Corean Reynolds explains what it entails. Officially Boston’s director of the nightlife economy, Ms Reynolds can sometimes be found standing outside a bar at 2am, watching how staff manage crowds spilling into the street. When residents complain about noise, she may be nearby with a decibel meter, with a plan not to kill the buzz but measure it. “I’m like, hey, actually it didn’t seem that loud,” she says she tells them. Her task is, essentially, to help more people have fun without making nearby residents miserable. She is the first person to hold the job in Boston and one of a rising number of officials across the country tasked with livening up life after dark.
paragraphs.zh: “夜生活沙皇”这个职位听起来几乎有点享乐主义色彩。可当科里安·雷诺兹解释它到底要做什么时，情况就不一样了。雷诺兹女士的正式头衔是波士顿夜间经济主管；有时凌晨2点，你会在一家酒吧门外看到她，观察工作人员如何管理涌到街上的人群。居民投诉噪音时，她可能就在附近，手里拿着分贝仪；她的计划不是扼杀热闹气氛，而是把它测量出来。她说自己会告诉居民：“我会说，嘿，其实听起来并没有那么吵。”从本质上说，她的任务是帮助更多人玩得开心，同时不让附近居民苦不堪言。她是波士顿第一个担任这一职位的人，也是全美越来越多负责让夜间生活活跃起来的官员之一。

paragraphs.en: For decades cities were interested mainly in enforcing strict rules, some dating to Prohibition, on pubs, music venues and dance halls. Now roughly a dozen American municipalities, from Iowa City to Philadelphia, have an official responsible for promoting the nightlife economy.
paragraphs.zh: 几十年来，城市主要关心的是对酒吧、音乐场所和舞厅执行严格规定，其中一些规定可追溯到禁酒令时期。如今，从艾奥瓦城到费城，美国大约十几个市政当局都设有一名负责推动夜间经济的官员。

paragraphs.en: This evolution in thinking can be seen in New York. A measure known as the Cabaret Law, which required venues to obtain special licences before allowing more than three people to dance, was introduced in 1926. In the 1990s Rudy Giuliani, then the city’s mayor, revived the law as part of his administration’s “Broken Windows” strategy, an approach to policing that sought to deter serious crime by cracking down on minor offences.
paragraphs.zh: 这种思路的演变可以在纽约看到。一项名为《歌舞厅法》的措施于1926年出台，要求场所在允许三人以上跳舞前必须取得特别许可证。20世纪90年代，时任纽约市长鲁迪·朱利安尼重新启用这项法律，将其作为其政府“破窗”策略的一部分；这种治安治理方式试图通过严厉打击轻微违法行为来遏制严重犯罪。

paragraphs.en: The law was finally repealed in 2017, and the city’s first nightlife mayor, Ariel Palitz, was appointed to her post the year after. Her office set up mediation services to help residents and business owners resolve noise complaints (Ms Palitz cautions that the correct term is “sound—not noise”). The prior system was “purely punitive”, she says, with each complaint automatically dispatching police. Her office also assisted bars, clubs and restaurants navigate permits and city bureaucracy. But enacting change has been arduous. Until May applicants for liquor licences in New York City still had to notify state officials if they wanted to allow dancing.
paragraphs.zh: 这项法律最终于2017年被废除，纽约市第一位夜生活市长阿丽尔·帕利茨在次年获任。她的办公室设立了调解服务，帮助居民和商家解决噪声投诉（帕利茨女士提醒说，正确的说法是“声音，而不是噪音”）。她说，此前的制度“完全是惩罚性的”，每一次投诉都会自动派警察出动。她的办公室还帮助酒吧、俱乐部和餐馆办理许可，并应对市政官僚程序。但推动改变一直十分艰难。直到5月以前，纽约市的酒类许可证申请者如果想允许跳舞，仍必须通知州政府官员。

paragraphs.en: For officials like Ms Reynolds, the biggest limits to her agenda remain red tape surrounding liquor licences, zoning and permitting. Boston is one of many cities that caps the number of liquor licences well below market demand. But in 2024, after Ms Reynolds pushed for change, the city created 225 new licences. According to the local government, it was “the single largest addition to Boston’s liquor licence quota since the end of Prohibition”.
paragraphs.zh: 对雷诺兹女士这样的官员来说，她议程面临的最大限制仍是围绕酒类许可证、分区规划和许可审批的繁文缛节。波士顿是许多城市之一，其酒类许可证数量上限远低于市场需求。但在2024年，经雷诺兹女士推动改革后，该市新增了225张许可证。当地政府称，这是“自禁酒令结束以来，波士顿酒类许可证配额单次最大规模的增加”。

paragraphs.en: Other cities are experimenting with new approaches that, if successful, could spread. San Francisco has created entertainment zones where patrons may consume alcohol on streets and sidewalks. Boston and some neighbouring municipalities have done the same as part of the World Cup. The tournament has become an opportunity to test looser rules, with several host cities temporarily extending bar hours. Meanwhile Austin, Texas, known for its live music, has adopted a policy that protects venues from noise complaints by occupants of new residential buildings, putting the onus on developers to provide adequate soundproofing.
paragraphs.zh: 其他城市正在尝试新做法，如果成功，可能会推广开来。旧金山设立了娱乐区，顾客可以在街道和人行道上饮酒。波士顿及一些邻近市镇也借世界杯之机采取了同样做法。这项赛事已成为测试更宽松规则的机会，多个主办城市临时延长了酒吧营业时间。与此同时，以现场音乐闻名的得克萨斯州奥斯汀通过了一项政策，保护演出场所免受新建住宅楼住户的噪声投诉，把提供充分隔音措施的责任放在开发商身上。

paragraphs.en: Smaller cities are following suit. Syracuse, a college town of 145,000 in upstate New York, recently announced plans to hire a nightlife co-ordinator. A thriving after-dark scene, city officials hope, will keep young professionals from moving away. It may also create jobs not just in hospitality and entertainment, but in sanitation, transport and security, as well as generate more tax revenue. At a time when remote work has left downtown areas quieter, encouraging nightlife is one way to sustain commercial activity after office hours while giving people more reasons to gather in person.
paragraphs.zh: 较小的城市也在跟进。雪城是纽约州北部一座拥有14.5万人口的大学城，最近宣布计划聘请一名夜生活协调员。市政官员希望，一个繁荣的夜间场景能留住年轻专业人士，避免他们迁往他处。它还可能创造就业岗位，不仅限于餐饮接待和娱乐行业，还包括环卫、交通和安保领域，并带来更多税收收入。在远程办公让市中心变得更安静之际，鼓励夜生活是在办公时间之后维持商业活动的一种方式，同时也给人们更多理由线下相聚。

paragraphs.en: Nightlife czars have a different challenge now, too. Between 2003 and 2023 data from the Bureau of Labour Statistics’ American Time Use Survey shows that the average amount of time 18- to 30-year-olds spent socialising each day fell by 38%. Andreina Seijas, an international nightlife consultant, says her industry is now in a war against the couch. In Boston, Ms Reynolds’s mission to reclaim the night continues. Next on her to-do list is persuading the city’s public-transport operator to run later into the night. The job of a nightlife czar is not always fun, but hopefully the cities they oversee will be. ■
paragraphs.zh: 如今，夜生活沙皇们还面临另一项挑战。美国劳工统计局“美国时间使用调查”的数据显示，2003年至2023年间，18至30岁人群平均每天用于社交的时间下降了38%。国际夜生活顾问安德烈娜·塞哈斯说，她所在的行业如今正在与沙发作战。在波士顿，雷诺兹女士夺回夜晚的使命仍在继续。她待办清单上的下一项，是说服该市公共交通运营机构把运营时间延长到更晚。夜生活沙皇的工作并不总是有趣，但希望他们所管理的城市会变得有趣。■

paragraphs.en: Stay on top of American politics with The US in brief, our daily newsletter with fast analysis of the most important political news, and Checks and Balance, a weekly note that examines the state of American democracy and the issues that matter to voters.
paragraphs.zh: 通过我们的每日简报《The US in brief》持续掌握美国政治动态，快速分析最重要的政治新闻；也可阅读每周通讯《Checks and Balance》，了解美国民主的状况以及选民关心的议题。
